description_long: This object is a vintage handheld flashlight with a cylindrical, ribbed body and a wide, flared head. The body is primarily a worn reddish-brown color with metallic accents, and it features a prominent black and red sliding switch on the side for operation. The head of the flashlight is circular with a textured rim, and the lens is recessed, protecting the bulb inside. The end cap at the rear is slightly darker and appears to be removable, likely for battery replacement. The surface shows signs of wear and age, with visible scuffs and patina, suggesting frequent use. This flashlight is designed for portability and would commonly be found in garages, workshops, or carried by individuals in urban environments.
description: A vintage, cylindrical handheld flashlight with a ribbed reddish-brown body, flared head, and a sliding switch on the side. The surface is worn and metallic, with a recessed lens and removable end cap.
description_view_0: Side view showing the ribbed body, flared head, and the sliding switch on top.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the circular head, lens, and the switch centered on the body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the switch visible and the ribbed texture running along the body.
description_view_3: Head-on view showing the recessed lens and the textured rim of the flashlight's head.
